He drew parallels with India's stance on illegal migration, emphasizing the US aims to secure its borders. Irfan Pathan expressed his surprise, while India captain Shreyas Iyer vowed to bounce back in the next match.

The incident, which occurred in Medak district, led to the arrest of the husband and his mother.  The victim's mother alleged continuous harassment, prompting police to register a case of cruelty and abetment of suicide under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

Despite Krishna's wicket-taking ability, his high economy rate in T20Is, conceding 11.5 runs per over, has become a significant worry for the team.  Krishna will aim for a better performance in the next match as India seeks to level the series.

description: In the first T20I against Ireland, he bowled only one over, introduced in the 16th over of the innings, and later scored 9 off 12 balls after coming in at No. 6.

title: Can India break its habit of self-medication?
description: India grapples with widespread self-medication, a habit deeply ingrained in its healthcare culture. While responsible self-care for minor ailments is encouraged, the unsupervised use of prescription drugs, especially antibiotics, fuels antimicrobial resistance. Recent government regulations on cough syrups signal a push for safer medicine use, but experts emphasize the need for improved healthcare access and public awareness to break this dangerous dependency.

The claims have drawn sharp criticism from opposition leaders, who label it a "new model of corruption" and a conflict of interest within the government.

title: India’s family firms are booming — but who will lead them tomorrow?

However, a significant challenge looms: the next generation is increasingly opting for careers outside the family firm. While founders remain optimistic, only a small fraction of heirs wish to take the reins, signaling a critical need for evolving succession strategies to ensure these economic powerhouses continue to thrive.

Police seized materials matching exam questions, prompting the Maharashtra State Council of Examination to cancel the test for transparency. Both the Cockroach Janta Party and Congress have slammed the BJP-led state government over the incident, calling it "incapable" and the "Paper Leak Government."

GCL Season 4 Draft: Carlsen returns, Divya set for debut - all you need to know
description: The Global Chess League (GCL) Season 4 Player Draft will be held in Mumbai on June 29, with the tournament set to take place in Bengaluru from September 3-13. World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen returns after missing last season, while FIDE Women's World Cup champion Divya Deshmukh will make her debut. The player pool features 36 Grandmasters, including Viswanathan Anand and Ian Nepomniachtchi.

Eye on Pakistan & China: Anti-submarine Eurodrone spurs India's layered maritime defence push
description: The development of a new anti-submarine warfare drone, a joint venture between Japan and Europe, highlights the growing importance of unmanned platforms in maritime security. This development is particularly relevant for India, facing expanding submarine fleets from Pakistan and China. The article emphasizes persistent surveillance, manned-unmanned teaming with existing assets like P-8I aircraft, and cost-effective coverage as key takeaways for strengthening India's naval defence capabilities.

description: Sunday's matches will decide which teams from Group A reach the semi-finals. South Africa, on six points, will face Bangladesh, who have four points. India, also on six points, will then take on unbeaten Australia, who have eight points.

description: Despite laws and awareness campaigns, ragging persists in educational institutions, often dismissed as tradition. However, its impact on victims is severe, leading to emotional distress, trauma, and even tragic outcomes.  Universities should be havens for growth, not fear.  Embracing empathy and respect is crucial to creating safe campuses where all students can thrive and reach their full potential.

description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

Inside Nebo-UM: The Russian radar that could help India track China's J-20
description: The Indian Air Force has unveiled its Russian Nebo-UM Very High Frequency radar, a state-of-the-art long-range surveillance tool designed to spot stealth aircraft. This advancement is crucial, especially with the rising number of stealth fighters in neighboring nations. Although it's not a weapon, this radar’s early warning system is vital, significantly boosting India's air defense framework against sophisticated aerial threats.

description: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

Couple denied boarding despite valid passes, spent night at airport; airline to pay Rs 62,000
description: SpiceJet has been ordered by a Srinagar consumer commission to pay Rs 62,078 to a Haj couple denied boarding despite having valid passes. The airline, failing to appear, was found guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. The couple, returning from pilgrimage, faced a night at the airport and rebooked flights at a higher cost due to the airline's inexplicable refusal to let them board.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says
description: New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws.
